creator: riggs@rrr.de
text:
On Mon, 12. Nov 2007, at  4:00 +0700, Eugene Grosbein wrote
according to [Re: ports/117962: [patch] multimedia/mplayer: add an option for AMR audio codecs]:

> > Is this a particular patch to the ffmpeg version that comes with
> > mplayer rc1? What about mplayer rc2? Will these patches apply as well?
> 
> They should. By the way, currently amr.c refers to older version
> of tarballs but newer work just fine. It seems that API it well declared.
> Again, there is no patching of mplayer sources really. Mplayer's configure
> script just looks if there are sources for AMR codecs in place;

This is different now. The rc2 version expects to find external libs
and links to it. The mplayer developers expect us to follow this link,
http://www.penguin.cz/~utx/amr
and use these tarballs to get amr running. It seems we need to create a
new port that manages the amr libs. Could you have a look at this
please?

As a reference, this is my current port update to the rc2 port:
http://www.rrr.de/~riggs/mplayer/m20071118.tar.bz2

It would be great if amr fixes/patches worked with the ports in this
tarball as rc2 will probably be committed to the ports tree soonish.

Regards,
Riggs

-- 
- "[...] I talked to the computer at great length and
-- explained my view of the Universe to it" said Marvin.
--- And what happened?" pressed Ford.
---- "It committed suicide." said Marvin.
